sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation found in sources

special_mechanics.name: Snott Parachute/Swing
special_mechanics.description: Jim's friend Snott can be used as a parachute upon jumping or to stick to and swing from slimy green surfaces, enabling descent control and traversal

special_mechanics.name: Inflated Head Float
special_mechanics.description: In 'Inflated Head' level, Jim's head inflates like a balloon causing him to float upward; sharp objects cause him to fall back to start

special_mechanics.name: Pocket Rocket Isometric Flight
special_mechanics.description: In 'The Flyin' King' level, Jim rides a pocket rocket with directional control; operates as isometric shooter mechanic

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Qualitative data only. Sources document gameplay mechanics and level design extensively but provide no quantitative physics parameters (velocities, accelerations, gravity values). Movement is heavily level-dependent with multiple specialized mechanics rather than consistent core physics. SNES version features weapon switching and alternate backgrounds compared to Genesis version.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Earthworm Jim 2 (SNES)
